findings.evidence: Convolution.lean:35-38 declares the source C as [Semiring C]; the distrib lemma adds [Bialgebra R B] with B (= the morphism domain) the only CommSemiring. H₃ is only a codomain/point-source here.
findings.issue: [CommSemiring H₃] is stronger than needed. H₃ never appears as a bialgebra-morphism domain — only as a codomain (of ψ) and as the source of the points WithConv (H₃ →ₐ A), where Mathlib's convolution monoid and convMul_comp_bialgHom_distrib (Convolution.lean:79, which constrains only its domain B to CommSemiring) need just Semiring.

findings.evidence: FunctorOfPoints.lean:80 uses `[Semiring H] [_root_.HopfAlgebra R H]`; mapDomain_inv_apply only forces CommSemiring on the domain H₁ via convMul_comp_bialgHom_distrib.
findings.issue: [CommSemiring H₂] is too strong in the Hopf section. H₂ appears only as φ's codomain and the Hopf source of points; HopfAlgebra R H₂ and instGroup need only [Semiring H₂] (cf. FunctorOfPoints.lean:80, which builds the same group with a noncommutative Semiring source).

summary: mapDomain over-constrains the codomain bialgebras to CommSemiring. The reused Mathlib lemma convMul_comp_bialgHom_distrib requires commutativity only of the morphism's domain; the codomain/point-source needs only Semiring, as the neighboring noncommutative-source API (FunctorOfPoints) already exploits.
